methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Velo3D is a public-stage company in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector that has raised over $300 million to date. The addressable peer universe consists of 12 companies operating in the same sector, comprising 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 identifiable private peers including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

The peer-median valuation method proved inapplicable to this analysis. Despite identifying 12 same-sector peers, the dataset contained insufficient datapoints meeting narrow filtering criteria—no peers matched on both stage and valuation tier simultaneously, and fewer than three comps survived any single filter combination. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of business models, scale, and technology maturity across the broader Robotics & Manufacturing universe.

As a result, this analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation range at this time. Valuation determination would require either: (1) expansion of the comparable set through acquisition of pricing data on the private peer cohort, (2) alternative methodologies such as DCF or precedent transaction analysis with documented assumptions, or (3) acceptance of the public market price as the marginal reference point. The key uncertainty remains the relevant peer tier—whether Velo3D benchmarks closer to early-stage private roboticists or to mature, profitable public manufacturers.
